item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had no idea what a “reciprocal pump” was until I was able to infer from context that it’s some kind of oil pump. If you could find a CC-compliant image of one it would probably, based on the comments here, really improve the page’s rating.

I agree with Warpzone that nonanomalous political shenanigans in Latin America would not be sufficient to prevent the foundation from responding to a Keter-level threat like this one.

In other news, ABCA-14 must be the worst damn place to work in the whole Foundation. Between this thing and SCP-058 there’ve been at least three containment breaches with body counts in the triple digits, plus a whole slew of incidents with SCP-939.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Six years later, read the whole article and I’m still pretty nonplussed about it. It takes too long to get to a point where you can really grasp what’s going on and by the time you get to that point you’re almost to the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's basically the protomolecule from Expanse, but in Foundation-verse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
About Addendum 2068-12: Really? We can infiltrate a dozen GOIs that rely on using anomalous objects, we can amnesticize the entire planet, we can build a friggin' moon base by shoving materials and personnel through a kiddie pool, and we can reboot the entire human race including memories, personalities, and personal histories millions of years after the fact… but we can't put eyes on the ground in a tin-pot banana republic?

I mean… really?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imagine Isis got this thing. *Shudders

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I suggest replacing, "more versions of itself" with, "additional instances of itself," to achieve a more clinical tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It didn't really do anything to pique my interest, and I dowvoted about halfway through because I was bored and nothing interesting was going on. Maybe something amazing happens in the second half to redeem it, but it took way too long to get there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh… learned something new from Echo. Unknown devices are disrupted, not detonated. Fixed that.

Shows what you learn only by paying attention to the news talking about security blowing up abandoned luggage.

item.title: A discussion and explanation. Spoilers.
item.content:encoded:
This was a discussion I wanted to share the explanation for…

That's a great backstory and I love it. I had a feeling that the pump was intending to be a pain in the ass.

Precisely. And the pump, being sapient, knows its job.

My only gripes are now, is why have a whole infected facility made for testing and containing an SCP that has aerosol germs that can fuck shit up with the perssonel? I see that the site contains other SCPs, but are we allowing -1 to make contact with them, with unknown results? Is there no easier way to contain this? Also, I'm not sure if anyone else figured out what was going on. At least, not that I know of. All I'm seeing is them saying its a brainy hostile pump.

A few people have. I figure some of the upvotes have, and I have talked it over with others. It's why I added the collapsible addendum.

It is delicious spoiler!
You must read it.

And it's the reason why testing went from clean conditions to using MOPP-4 gear (Which is those rubber body suits with their own breathing apparatus) to a complete ban on testing. And that's why I showed the crossed out results, to show the story of the escalation. At first, they only thought it affected electronics, hence the command to work in clean room conditions, using Foundation techs, and no electronics within a radius. They expanded the radius, then spotted odd biology effects which lead to the MOPP-4 and the D-Class.

This is subtle, so missing it isn't a ding on you. I designed it to be subtle, and also a "Take That!" against zombies, among other things. Like the fact that the Overseer Council just pulled a Chernobyl clean up by turning the survivors into unpersons, hiding this fact to preserve morale.

A truly evil idea would be for the survivors to be performing experiments on themselves, since they have to be isolated in their own little area. And the paranoia at ABCA-14 over whether anything remains even after they cooked the place. And the desire to know what the fuck is going on here?

Playing the Foundation and its tropes. Too bad Dr. Clef is busy being enigmatic somewhere else this week.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The ammonia question is actually a pretty simple one. It burns, and as noted, fire and heat break down 2068-1. The ammonia is leftover from treating the nitrogen atmosphere, so it's another means to destroy any potential taint.

Now, why is it doing all of this? There is actually a very good reason behind it, but it's not obvious. The SCP is written from the Foundation's viewpoint, and honestly they are just as baffled to its motive as most anyone would be.

You may assume that is rather the point, from the Chaos Pump's point of view.

Thanks for reading and questioning!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like I'm missing something here: the thing's purpose.

So it's a biological computer hosting a series of biological nanomachines which can then convert organic matter into other substances (and through this, somehow control people). It appears to be sapient, given its strong attempts to breach containment.

Why is it doing all of this? What does it think it should be doing? Why does ammonia help keep it contained?

One final note is that I don't like the crossing out in the containment procedures. I get that it's become more dangerous, but there should be a better way to do this.

No vote for now, but I'll be watching.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I added a collapsible for the autopsy and vivisection reports. I may add more information, although I am reluctant to remove all of the mystery of the artifact.

The catch is that the Foundation is reacting to the device, and there are reasons for that. I'm hoping that I can keep this article from the viewpoint of the Foundation, so as not to "spoil" it, but I may need to add in one other thing to seal it. It's a challenge, I think, since I suspect my problem is that I'm not being candid enough.

This may just be one that not everyone is going to get, alas. Or not everyone will like, even when they get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It does feel disorganized to me, but I like the concept. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just feels confused. It's a pump containing a brain that controls people. I don't see anything that interesting. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like something is missing, maybe what this thing does to people, or how it does it. That note from Doctor Blackbox could probably stand to be stated in a more clinical tone, rather than a personal observation. And what's with all the "shall"s?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Look at it this way: The Foundation doesn't know what this thing is, exactly. They know it's alive, has biological output, and it's contagious and does horrible things to people in what appears to be a desire to reproduce. What they don't know is what or how it is eating, whether it's intelligent, what its motives are, if any, or anything other that whatever it is, it's alive, in a oil pump, has biological components, and appears to have been manufactured.

I suggested to Aelanna some tags for it, which might help from a story-telling view, but to the Foundation, this thing is a mystery… and that's on purpose because it's an underlying tenet to the story. Because if the Foundation knew what it was, they'd destroy it utterly.

Most likely… because the Foundation are the Hoarders of the supernatural world here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the fact you changed the sentence, but I still think the description still lacks clarity. If this thing can infect people and make them into its drones, that would be made Exceptionally clear immediately. These reports serve as warnings to the people interacting with these things so as to keep personnel safe and the object contained. I would have opened the description with "SCP-2068 is a biological computer capable of infecting individuals and converting them into all sorts of dangerous business etc, etc, omg look out!" You wouldn't want to mince words with something like this, you know? That's why warning labels tend to be right to the point. "High Voltage," not "It is surmised that this object can transfer electrons to any object that comes in contact with it." I understand you were building up the story, but I think the addendums really pack enough of a punch to be a lot more straightforward to begin with.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I had no idea what it was. I got it was a pump or a computer or something at best but after carefully rereading the article…I got it

It did scare me though and I almost lost my lunch at the memetic effect so to speak. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmmm… Okay, I can see that. Rephrased the first sentence, so that the Reciprocal Pump is the SCP, since it is basically the thing's exoskeleton. The clam shell is still part of the clam, after all, even though it's not alive itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, Scanny. I might see about using this as a lead in for the way I think the CI should play. They are seriously in need of a vision.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the first sentence of the description shouldn't be about where it was housed, but instead what it is. "SCP-2068 is a biological computer of unknown origin blah blah blah."
The containment procedures really drew me in, so when I got the description and was confused about what precisely this thing was, it sort of crashed the article for me. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a biological computer housed in an oil pump that attempts to draw in resources and reproduce.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice escalation, charmingly old-school, leaves enough to the imagination. I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Description doesn't tell me what it is.

Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My first SCP in a long time, the Chaos Pump. I had it in mind originally for the SCP-2000 contest, but it wasn't ready to play.

Hope you like it.
